---
title: "LRPAL01d: Aligned-40-week dose sensitivity variant for word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
factor_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "factor_summary.csv")
cohort_marginal = pd.read_csv(_here / "cohort_marginal.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPAL01d is the **dose sensitivity variant** of [LRPAL01](../lrpal01/) for word
reading (`ewrswr`): the same onset-aligned per-protocol ANCOVA, with the
cumulative sessions delivered over the aligned window added as a covariate
(`gamma_dose`).

::: {.callout-important title="Dose is a collider — sensitivity only"}

Cumulative sessions are a **collider** on the DAG (a descendant of both group —
the immediate arm accrues sessions earlier — and ability — more able / available
children attend more), so conditioning on dose can open a back-door. This model
is therefore a **sensitivity** read, **not** a primary adjustment set: interpret
`gamma_dose` as a within-arm dose-response *association*, expected to be weak or
inconclusive once onset baseline, age-at-onset and ability are in the model, and
never as a causal dose effect. As in LRPAL01 the cohort term is non-randomised, so
**every** coefficient here is an association.

:::

## Model

$$
W_{\text{post(aligned)}} \sim \operatorname{BetaBinomial}(n, \operatorname{expit}(\eta), \kappa),\quad
\eta = \alpha + \beta_{\text{cohort}}\,\text{immediate}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(W_{\text{onset}})
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{onset,std}} + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks})
  + \gamma_{\text{dose}}z(\text{sessions}).
$$

One row per child (no random intercept). Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.png){width=24%}
![tau (cohort)](prior_tau.png){width=24%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.png){width=24%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.png){width=24%}

## Prior-predictive check

![Prior predictive](prior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior-predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Findings

### Factor summary (all associations)

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary
```

![Adjusted-association coefficients (forest, reference line at 0)](association_forest.png){.lightbox}

`gamma_dose` is the within-arm cumulative-session association (the sensitivity
term of interest); every `role` is **association**. `mean`/`lo`/`hi` = posterior
mean + equal-tailed 95% CrI; `prob_positive` = $P(\text{coef} > 0)$.

### Cohort marginal (per-protocol, NOT randomised)

```{python}
# | echo: false
cohort_marginal
```

The items-scale immediate-vs-wait-list difference at the aligned endpoints, with
dose adjusted — a per-protocol association, not the randomised treatment effect.

**Reading these tables.** Per the project's reporting conventions (`METHODS.md`,
"Interpret"), lead with the **interval**, not the point estimate, and read
`prob_positive` / `prob_trt_pos` as evidence of **direction, not magnitude**. At this
design's small per-arm sample (~26–28) a point estimate is on average
magnitude-inflated (a Type-M / winner's-curse effect), so the posterior mean is not a
number to quote on its own. A ROPE-anchored **magnitude** read (`P(|effect| ≥ δ)`
against a minimally-important difference, as now reported for the randomised ITT
suite) is **deliberately deferred** for this per-protocol family — applying a
"meaningful benefit" ROPE to a confounded association would mis-frame it; see
`notes/202606261343-lrpal-aligned-design.md`.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
